eventMetadata.context_description: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair expires May 15, aligning with his April 29 announcement that he will step aside from the role while remaining a board governor until 2028, driving trader consensus to 96.5% odds for departure in the May 15–22 window. The Senate's 54-45 confirmation of President Trump's nominee Kevin Warsh on May 13 further cements an orderly transition, as incoming chairs historically assume duties immediately upon outgoing term end, reflecting standard Fed protocol amid no reported holds or delays. This commanding position stems from verifiable procedural milestones and Powell's public commitment, with the wisdom of crowds pricing in minimal disruption. Realistic challenges include unforeseen legal hurdles, health events, or bipartisan Senate objections stalling Warsh's swearing-in, though these face high barriers given recent momentum.

markets.question: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United Kingdom government formally designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Clausura 2026 Liga FPD final, top-seeded CS Herediano—boasting the league's most prolific attack with 38 points from 18 regular-season games—face Deportivo Saprissa, who hold the best defense and earned their spot by ousting Municipal Liberia in the semifinals despite absences of key players Mariano, Fidel Vargas, and Paradela. Saprissa relied on Abraham Madriz's impact to advance, mirroring their 2-1 home win over Herediano in March. The first leg kicks off Wednesday at Estadio Ricardo Saprissa, favoring the hosts' home form in this storied rivalry, while Herediano eyes series victory for automatic championship. Recent semifinal triumphs underscore both sides' momentum, with trader sentiment hinging on defensive resilience versus offensive firepower in the two-legged aggregate.

events.title: Odisha FC vs. Punjab FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for Saturday, May 16, 2026 between Odisha FC and Punjab FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Odisha FC holds a slim edge as home side at Kalinga Stadium, but trader consensus prices reflect Punjab FC's stronger recent form offsetting that advantage in a relegation-threatened clash for the Juggernauts. Punjab sit 7th with 14 points from eight matches (4W-2D-2L), fresh off a 3-0 dismantling of Inter Kashi on April 20, showcasing solid defense and counter-attacks, while Odisha languish 11th on six points from eight (1W-3D-4L), stung by a stoppage-time loss to Kerala Blasters on April 23 and a 1-1 draw with Mohammedan. Their February 1-1 head-to-head stalemate underscores the draw's appeal, with no major injuries altering lineups amid both teams' mid-table table positioning and playoff irrelevance.

markets.question: Will the 2026 trade deficit be between 900B and 1T?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026, as reported by the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) and the US Census Bureau (USCB) in the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026, expected to be released in February 2027.

Upon publication, the specified release will be made available at: https://www.bea.gov/news/current-releases

The relevant figure may be found in the annual summary under “Exports, Imports, and Balance (exhibit 1)”. Changes in the BEA or USCB’s reporting format will not disqualify a relevant published figure from counting.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 from the US Bureau of Economic Analysis and the US Census Bureau. If this release is not published by April 30, 2027 ET, another credible source on the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 will be chosen.

Note: any revisions to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 made after the publication of the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 will not be considered.

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026, as reported by the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) and the US Census Bureau (USCB) in the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026, expected to be released in February 2027.

Upon publication, the specified release will be made available at: https://www.bea.gov/news/current-releases

The relevant figure may be found in the annual summary under “Exports, Imports, and Balance (exhibit 1)”. Changes in the BEA or USCB’s reporting format will not disqualify a relevant published figure from counting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2026 US trade deficit tilts toward 800–900 billion at 42%, edging out 900 billion–1 trillion at 36%, driven by mixed signals from Bureau of Economic Analysis data released May 5 showing March's goods and services gap widening 4.4% to $60.3 billion amid surging imports outpacing exports. Year-to-date through March, the deficit plunged 55% from 2025's pace, with trailing 12-month totals at $776 billion, but recent upticks reflect robust US consumer demand and capital goods inflows. Uncertainty from a federal court striking down key Trump-era "Liberation Day" tariffs last week, plus ongoing Trump-Xi talks on potential cuts to $30 billion in imports, keeps the race tight; upcoming April data (due mid-June) and tariff appeal outcomes could widen the gap toward lower or higher bins depending on import trends and trade policy resolutions.

markets.question: Tshisekedi out as President of the DRC by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Félix Tshisekedi ceases to be President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Félix Tshisekedi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Félix Tshisekedi and the government of the Democratic Republic of the Congo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Félix Tshisekedi remains firmly entrenched midway through his second term, constitutionally secured until December 2028 following his contested 2023 re-election. Trader consensus at 91% "No" reflects this stability, reinforced by his May 6 press conference signaling openness to a third term if public demand arises via constitutional revision and potential 2028 election delays amid eastern DRC insecurity from M23 rebel advances. Opposition leaders decry these remarks as a "constitutional coup," but no snap elections, viable no-confidence mechanisms, or coup threats have materialized. Recent Doha ceasefire terms with M23 and Tshisekedi's international engagements, including US talks on minerals, underscore his control absent major disruptions.

markets.question: Will Alexander Albon be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli holds the slimmest of edges in trader consensus for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship after securing three consecutive Grand Prix victories in China, Japan, and Miami, where he converted pole to win ahead of Lando Norris's P2 and teammate George Russell's P4, extending his points lead to 20 over Russell. Mercedes' dominant race pace and qualifying supremacy under new power unit regs have propelled their intra-team duel to the forefront, with Antonelli's rookie poise outperforming expectations while Russell's experience keeps odds tight at 37.5% versus 29.5%. McLaren's Norris (11.5%) gains traction from consistent podiums amid Red Bull's struggles dropping Max Verstappen to 6.3%, but 20+ races remain for upsets via pit strategy or reliability issues.

Historical head-to-head favors Barcelona heavily (17 wins in 20).

eventMetadata.context_description: **Incumbent Gov. Ned Lamont's commanding position in trader consensus at 94.5% for Connecticut's August 11 Democratic gubernatorial primary stems from his strong incumbency advantage, consistent polling dominance, and approval ratings in the 50s-60s.** Recent University of New Hampshire surveys, including an April poll showing Lamont at 52% to State Rep. Josh Elliott's 18% among likely primary voters, underscore limited gains for the progressive challenger despite his left-flank criticism on taxing the wealthy and social services. Elliott's bid intensifies at the May 16 Democratic endorsing convention in Hartford, where 15% delegate support guarantees primary ballot access; otherwise, he must petition signatures. Realistic challenges include a convention upset enabling Elliott to mobilize turnout, or late-breaking Lamont scandal, gaffe, or health issue shifting odds before early voting.

eventMetadata.context_description: OGC Nice, sitting 15th in Ligue 1 with 31 points after a 1-1 home draw against Lens in matchweek 32, face a critical home clash against relegated bottom-side FC Metz on May 17 at Allianz Riviera. Desperate to secure survival and avoid the relegation playoff—especially ahead of their pivotal away trip to 16th-placed Auxerre—the hosts hold a strong historical edge, unbeaten in recent home fixtures versus Metz and boasting just two losses in 19 such encounters. However, injuries plague Nice, with forward Elye Wahi (thigh), defender Moïse Bombito (lower leg), and others sidelined, mirroring Metz's extensive absences including Habib Diallo (hamstring) and multiple defenders. Metz's poor away form and demotivation post-relegation could favor a low-scoring affair, though Nice's leaky defense (58 goals conceded) adds upset risk.

events.title: Wolverhampton Wanderers FC vs. Fulham FC - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the Premier League game between Wolverhampton Wanderers FC and Fulham FC, scheduled for May 17, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Wolverhampton Wanderers, already relegated in 20th place with 18 points from 36 matches and a -41 goal difference, host mid-table Fulham in their penultimate Premier League fixture at Molineux on May 17. Wolves' recent form remains dismal, including a 1-1 draw versus Sunderland on May 2 and losses like 0-1 to Tottenham, hampered by goalkeeper injuries to José Sá (ankle) and Sam Johnstone (shoulder, mid-May return doubtful) plus Enso González (knee). Fulham, safer with around 44 points, miss Ryan Sessegnon (muscle), Alex Iwobi (hamstring), and Kevin Santos (leg), all mid-May doubts. Player props favor low-output scenarios given Wolves' scoring woes and mutual defensive absences, with historical head-to-heads averaging 2.67 goals.

markets.question: Will Cristiano Ronaldo announce his retirement in 2026?
markets.description:
This polymarket will resolve to "Yes" if Cristiano Ronaldo officially announces his retirement from club soccer by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To resolve "Yes", the announcement must clearly state that Ronaldo is retiring from club-level professional soccer. The announcement must come from Ronaldo himself or his official representatives.

Retirement from international soccer alone will not qualify. 

The retirement must be intended to take effect immediately or prior to the start of the next club season following the announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Cristiano Ronaldo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cristiano Ronaldo's elite recent form in the Saudi Pro League, including his landmark 100th goal for Al-Nassr in just 105 appearances as of early May 2026, underpins trader consensus pricing "No" at 88% implied probability for a 2026 retirement announcement. The 41-year-old Portugal captain, contracted with Al-Nassr through June 2027, continues scoring prolifically—netting in late April wins during an extended club win streak—while showing no injury concerns or diminished output. His November 2025 CNN statement framing the 2026 World Cup as his international finale, paired with January reports of a potential final 2026/27 club campaign, signals commitment beyond this year, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in a market focused on announcement timing amid sustained peak performance.

events.title: Getafe CF vs. RCD Mallorca
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for Wednesday, May 13, 2026 between Getafe CF and RCD Mallorca.

eventMetadata.context_description: Getafe CF's commanding 3-1 home victory over RCD Mallorca in their La Liga matchup at Coliseum Alfonso Pérez has driven trader consensus to 100% on a Getafe win, as the market resolves based on the official full-time scoreline. Martín Satriano's first-half brace (14' assisted by Nyom, 41') put the 7th-placed hosts firmly in control, followed by Zaid Romero's 63' header from a Luis Milla assist, with Mallorca's 17th-placed side—fighting relegation—managing only a late Omar Mascarell consolation (65'). Pre-match favoritism stemmed from Getafe's superior standing, strong home record, and Mallorca's poor away form, leaving no viable scenarios to challenge the outcome post-whistle.

events.title: Germany vs. Finland
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for Sunday, May 31, 2026 between Germany and Finland.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany enters the May 31 international friendly in Mainz as overwhelming trader favorites at 85% implied probability, driven by their superior FIFA ranking, home advantage at MEWA Arena, and recent dominant form including Florian Wirtz's masterclass—two goals and two assists—in a thrilling victory over Switzerland six weeks ago. Head-to-head history favors Die Mannschaft, unbeaten against Finland with a 2-0 win in their last 2016 friendly, underscoring the quality gap. Despite Serge Gnabry's long-term muscle tear ruling him out for the 2025-26 season and recovery concerns for Jamal Musiala and Kai Havertz, Germany's squad depth maintains edge. Finland, hampered by Lucas Bergström's injury and lacking firepower, sees draw and win at 10% each, reflecting limited upset potential absent major German absences.
